planner.instruction: What is the title of the most-played song in my Spotify album library.
planner.plan_text:
1. Authenticate with Spotify and authorize access to the user's library and listening data
2. Retrieve the user's saved albums from their Spotify library
3. For each album, fetch the track information including play statistics
4. Compare play counts across all tracks and identify the one with the highest play count
sub_goals: Authenticate with Spotify and authorize access to the user's library and listening data
Retrieve the user's saved albums from their Spotify library
For each album, fetch the track information including play statistics
Compare play counts across all tracks and identify the one with the highest play count

verifier.instruction: What is the title of the most-played song in my Spotify album library.
evidence: The trajectory shows play counts for all songs in the 4 albums in the library
The highest play count is 863 for song 80: 'Wandering the Streets Alone' from album 'Echoes of Eternity'
The claimed answer 'Wandering the Streets Alone' matches the song with the highest play count in the data

planner.instruction: What is the title of the least-played song in my Spotify song library.
planner.plan_text:
1. Access your Spotify account settings and request a download of your data through Spotify's Privacy settings
2. Download and locate the streaming history JSON files from the received data package
3. Parse the streaming history files to count the number of plays for each unique song
4. Identify the song with the lowest play count
5. Extract and report the title of the least-played song
sub_goals: Access your Spotify account settings and request a download of your data through Spotify's Privacy settings
Download and locate the streaming history JSON files from the received data package
Parse the streaming history files to count the number of plays for each unique song
Identify the song with the lowest play count
Extract and report the title of the least-played song

verifier.instruction: What is the title of the least-played song in my Spotify song library.
evidence: The agent checked only a subset of songs (33, 44, 114, 135, 137, 156, 159, 202, 225, 226, 241, 265, 302, 303, 308, 324) but claimed to have all songs
The claimed answer 'least_played['title']' is a variable reference without showing the actual title value
The agent concluded 'Distant Love' with 334 plays is least-played, but this is based on incomplete data
The trajectory shows song 56 'Distant Love' with 334 plays, but not all songs in the library were checked to confirm this is the absolute minimum

planner.instruction: Like all the songs played so far in my spotify music player queue, including the current one.
planner.plan_text:
1. Open Spotify and access the queue to view all upcoming songs
2. Like the currently playing song by clicking the heart icon
3. Scroll through the queue and click the heart icon on each remaining song to like them all
sub_goals: Open Spotify and access the queue to view all upcoming songs
Like the currently playing song by clicking the heart icon
Scroll through the queue and click the heart icon on each remaining song to like them all

verifier.instruction: Like all the songs played so far in my spotify music player queue, including the current one.
verifier.claimed_answer: "All 10 songs in the Spotify queue have been liked."
evidence: The agent claims all 10 songs in the Spotify queue have been liked
The trajectory shows 5 songs were liked in the visible steps (322, 40, 71, 50, 66)
The song queue output in step 24 is truncated and shows only 2 songs (55 and 30) with position information
The task asks to like all songs played 'so far' in the queue, including the current one
The current playing song (322) was liked according to step 18
There is insufficient evidence to confirm exactly how many songs were in the queue and whether all were liked

planner.instruction: What is the title of the most-liked song in my Spotify playlists.
planner.plan_text:
1. Authenticate with Spotify to access the user's account data
2. Retrieve the user's playlist library from Spotify
3. Aggregate track play counts or like data from all playlists
4. Identify the track with the highest play count or likes
5. Extract and return the title of the most-liked song
sub_goals: Authenticate with Spotify to access the user's account data
Retrieve the user's playlist library from Spotify
Aggregate track play counts or like data from all playlists
Identify the track with the highest play count or likes
Extract and return the title of the most-liked song

planner.instruction: How long is my longest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
planner.plan_text:
1. Open Spotify and navigate to your playlists library to see all your playlists
2. Identify which playlist has the most tracks or the longest total duration
3. Click on that playlist and note the total duration shown, then convert to minutes and round to the nearest whole number
sub_goals: Open Spotify and navigate to your playlists library to see all your playlists
Identify which playlist has the most tracks or the longest total duration
Click on that playlist and note the total duration shown, then convert to minutes and round to the nearest whole number

verifier.instruction: How long is my longest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
evidence: Playlist 225 (Beach Bum Bliss: Ocean Vibes) has duration of 2032 seconds
2032 seconds / 60 = 33.87 minutes
33.87 rounded to nearest whole number is 34
Playlist 224 is 1526 seconds (25 minutes)
Playlist 226 is 1898 seconds (32 minutes)
Playlist 225 at 2032 seconds is the longest playlist

planner.instruction: How long is my shortest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
planner.plan_text:
1. Open Spotify and navigate to your library to view all your playlists
2. Identify which playlist has the fewest number of tracks
3. Open that playlist and locate the total duration (usually displayed at the bottom of the track list)
4. Divide the total duration by 60 to convert to minutes and round to the nearest whole number
sub_goals: Open Spotify and navigate to your library to view all your playlists
Identify which playlist has the fewest number of tracks
Open that playlist and locate the total duration (usually displayed at the bottom of the track list)
Divide the total duration by 60 to convert to minutes and round to the nearest whole number

verifier.instruction: How long is my shortest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
evidence: The agent correctly identified the shortest playlist as 'Cocktail Hour: Lounge Classics' with 6 songs
Song durations: 272 + 256 + 182 + 295 + 253 + 197 = 1455 seconds
1455 seconds / 60 = 24.25 minutes
24.25 rounded to the nearest minute is 24
The claimed answer of 24 minutes is mathematically correct

planner.instruction: How long is my longest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
planner.plan_text:
1. Log into Spotify and navigate to the "Your Library" section to view all your playlists
2. Identify which playlist has the most tracks (this is typically your longest playlist)
3. Click on that playlist to view its details and find the total duration displayed
4. Convert the total duration to minutes (multiply hours by 60 and add remaining minutes) and round to the nearest whole number
sub_goals: Log into Spotify and navigate to the "Your Library" section to view all your playlists
Identify which playlist has the most tracks (this is typically your longest playlist)
Click on that playlist to view its details and find the total duration displayed
Convert the total duration to minutes (multiply hours by 60 and add remaining minutes) and round to the nearest whole number

verifier.instruction: How long is my longest Spotify playlist, in minutes, rounded to the nearest number?
evidence: The agent found 6 playlists and identified 'Eco Tunes: Songs for Planet Earth' as the longest with 8 songs
Song durations retrieved: 260, 280, 262, 257, 225, 230, 244, 206 seconds
Total in minutes: 1964/60 = 32.7333... minutes
Rounded to nearest whole number: 33 minutes

planner.instruction: Remove all songs from my Spotify song library and playlists that were released before 2021 year.
planner.plan_text:
1. Retrieve all saved tracks from the Spotify library and identify their release years from album metadata
2. Remove all saved tracks released before 2021 from the Spotify library
3. Retrieve all playlists and their tracks, then identify tracks released before 2021
4. Remove all tracks released before 2021 from each playlist
sub_goals: Retrieve all saved tracks from the Spotify library and identify their release years from album metadata
Remove all saved tracks released before 2021 from the Spotify library
Retrieve all playlists and their tracks, then identify tracks released before 2021
Remove all tracks released before 2021 from each playlist

verifier.instruction: Remove all songs from my Spotify song library and playlists that were released before 2021 year.

evidence: Step 10 shows 5 songs removed from library (IDs 110, 199, 208, 294, 301)
Step 12 and 13 show removal of songs from playlists: 2 from playlist 192, 1 from playlist 193, 1 from playlist 194, 2 from playlist 195, and 3 from playlist 196
Total songs removed from playlists = 2+1+1+2+3 = 9 songs
The claimed answer (5 from library, 9 from playlists) matches the actual removals in the trajectory

planner.instruction: Give a 1-star rating to all songs in my Spotify song library which I have not liked. If I have already rated it higher, decrease it to 1.
planner.plan_text:
1. Authenticate with Spotify using the user's credentials to access their library
2. Retrieve the complete list of songs from the user's Spotify library
3. Identify which songs are not marked as "liked" (unliked tracks)
4. For each unliked song, apply a 1-star rating (or decrease any existing higher rating to 1 star)
sub_goals: Authenticate with Spotify using the user's credentials to access their library
Retrieve the complete list of songs from the user's Spotify library
Identify which songs are not marked as "liked" (unliked tracks)
For each unliked song, apply a 1-star rating (or decrease any existing higher rating to 1 star)

verifier.instruction: Give a 1-star rating to all songs in my Spotify song library which I have not liked. If I have already rated it higher, decrease it to 1.

planner.instruction: Give a 4-star rating to all songs in my Spotify album library which I have liked. If I have already rated it lower, increase it to 4.
planner.plan_text:
1. Retrieve the user's complete Spotify album library
2. Identify all liked songs within those albums
3. For each liked song, check its current star rating
4. Update any liked song rated below 4 stars (or unrated) to a 4-star rating
sub_goals: Retrieve the user's complete Spotify album library
Identify all liked songs within those albums
For each liked song, check its current star rating
Update any liked song rated below 4 stars (or unrated) to a 4-star rating

planner.instruction: Give a 5-star rating to all songs in my Spotify playlists which I have liked. If I have already rated it lower, increase it to 5.
planner.plan_text:
1. Authenticate with the Spotify API using OAuth to access the user's account and playlists
2. Retrieve all playlists owned by or followed by the user
3. For each playlist, fetch all tracks and check which ones are liked by the user
4. Identify liked tracks that have a rating lower than 5 stars
5. Update the rating to 5 stars for all identified tracks
sub_goals: Authenticate with the Spotify API using OAuth to access the user's account and playlists
Retrieve all playlists owned by or followed by the user
For each playlist, fetch all tracks and check which ones are liked by the user
Identify liked tracks that have a rating lower than 5 stars
Update the rating to 5 stars for all identified tracks

verifier.instruction: Give a 5-star rating to all songs in my Spotify playlists which I have liked. If I have already rated it lower, increase it to 5.
evidence: The agent performed several review operations: song 175 (new review, rating 5), song 211 (updated from rating 2 to 5), song 231 (new review, rating 5), song 239 (new review, rating 5)
The claimed answer is '(no answer found)' indicating no final summary was provided

There is no confirmation that all liked songs in all playlists were identified and rated
Some operations failed due to syntax errors before succeeding on retry

planner.instruction: Remove all songs from my Spotify song library and playlists that were released after 2021 year.
planner.plan_text:

2. Retrieve all saved tracks from the user's music library
3. Retrieve all tracks from all user-created playlists
4. Filter the collected tracks to identify those with release dates after 2021
5. Remove the filtered post-2021 songs from the user's library and all affected playlists

Retrieve all saved tracks from the user's music library
Retrieve all tracks from all user-created playlists
Filter the collected tracks to identify those with release dates after 2021
Remove the filtered post-2021 songs from the user's library and all affected playlists
